cases.chiefComplaint: Right lower quadrant pain for 24 hours
cases.history: Patient reports gradual onset of periumbilical pain that migrated to right lower quadrant. Associated with nausea and one episode of vomiting. Low-grade fever. Anorexia present. Last menstrual period 2 weeks ago. No urinary symptoms.
cases.physicalExam: Appears uncomfortable. McBurney's point tenderness present. Positive Rovsing's sign. No rebound initially, but guarding present. Psoas sign positive.
cases.imaging: CT abdomen/pelvis: Appendiceal diameter 9mm with wall thickening >2mm. Moderate periappendiceal stranding. No abscess or perforation.
cases.correctDiagnosis: Acute Appendicitis (Grade III - Appendicitis with Localized Peritonitis)
cases.keyDifferentiator: Moderate periappendiceal inflammatory stranding on CT without perforation
cases.diagnosticCriteria: BJR 2011 Grade III: appendiceal diameter >6mm, wall thickening, moderate-severe periappendiceal stranding without wall defect
cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Боль в правой подвздошной области в течение 24 часов
cases.history_ru: Пациентка сообщает о постепенном начале околопупочной боли с последующей миграцией в правую подвздошную область. Сопровождается тошнотой и однократной рвотой. Отмечалась субфебрильная температура. Есть анорексия. Последняя менструация была 2 недели назад. Мочевых симптомов нет.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Выглядит испытывающей дискомфорт. Имеется болезненность в точке Мак-Бурнея. Положительный симптом Ровзинга. Изначально симптом Щёткина-Блюмберга отрицательный, но присутствует мышечная защита. Положительный симптом поясничной мышцы.
cases.imaging_ru: КТ брюшной полости и таза: диаметр аппендикса 9 мм, утолщение стенки >2 мм. Умеренные воспалительные изменения периаппендикулярной клетчатки. Абсцесса и перфорации нет.
cases.correctDiagnosis_ru: Острый аппендицит (III степень - аппендицит с локализованным перитонитом)

cases.chiefComplaint: Right upper abdominal pain and fever
cases.history: Patient reports gradual onset of right upper quadrant pain starting 8pm previous evening after eating Chinese takeout. Pain constant, lasted several hours. This morning felt better initially but pain recurred mid-morning and worsened throughout day. Episode of shaking chills in waiting room. Fever present. No vomiting but nausea present. Last bowel movement yesterday, normal. Two prior similar episodes: 6 months ago (lasted 3 hours, resolved) and 2 weeks ago (resolved overnight).
cases.physicalExam: Lying on right side, knees bent, obvious pain on face. Cooperative but preoccupied with pain. Right upper quadrant tenderness severe. Pain radiates to right shoulder blade. Murphy's sign difficult to assess due to severe tenderness.
cases.imaging: Right upper quadrant ultrasound: Dilated common bile duct (12mm), gallstones present, thickened gallbladder wall. Positive sonographic Murphy's sign.
cases.keyDifferentiator: Charcot's triad (fever, RUQ pain, jaundice) with dilated common bile duct
cases.diagnosticCriteria: OSCE 2010 source - Cholangitis with Charcot's triad
cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Боль в правом подреберье и лихорадка
cases.history_ru: Пациентка сообщает о постепенном начале боли в правом подреберье, начавшейся накануне вечером после еды. Боль постоянная, продолжалась несколько часов. Утром сначала стало легче, затем боль вернулась и усиливалась в течение дня. В приёмном покое возник эпизод потрясающего озноба. Есть лихорадка. Рвоты нет, но есть тошнота. Последний стул был вчера, обычный. Два похожих эпизода были ранее: 6 месяцев назад и 2 недели назад, оба самостоятельно прошли.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Лежит на правом боку с согнутыми коленями, на лице выраженная боль. Контактна, но сосредоточена на боли. Выраженная болезненность в правом подреберье. Боль иррадиирует в правую лопатку. Симптом Мёрфи трудно оценить из-за сильной болезненности.
cases.imaging_ru: УЗИ правого подреберья: расширенный общий желчный проток 12 мм, имеются камни в желчном пузыре, утолщение стенки желчного пузыря. Положительный сонографический симптом Мёрфи.
cases.additionalFindings_ru: Тёмная моча цвета чая, светлый стул

cases.chiefComplaint: Left lower abdominal pain for 2 days
cases.history: Patient reports gradual onset of left lower quadrant pain 2 days ago. Initially crampy, now constant. Associated with low-grade fever and chills. Some nausea, no vomiting. Last bowel movement 2 days ago. History of occasional constipation. No prior similar episodes. No rectal bleeding.
cases.physicalExam: Appears uncomfortable. Left lower quadrant tenderness with voluntary guarding. No rebound. Bowel sounds hypoactive. No palpable masses. CVA tenderness negative.
cases.imaging: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast: Sigmoid colon wall thickening, pericolonic fat stranding, small localized fluid collection adjacent to sigmoid colon. Diverticulosis present.
cases.keyDifferentiator: Sigmoid wall thickening with pericolonic stranding and small fluid collection on CT
cases.diagnosticCriteria: OSCE 2010 source - Diverticulitis with localized perforation
cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Боль в левой подвздошной области в течение 2 дней
cases.history_ru: Пациент сообщает о постепенном начале боли в левой подвздошной области 2 дня назад. Сначала боль была схваткообразной, затем стала постоянной. Сопровождается субфебрильной температурой и ознобом. Есть небольшая тошнота, рвоты нет. Последний стул был 2 дня назад. В анамнезе эпизодические запоры. Подобного ранее не было. Ректального кровотечения нет.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Выглядит испытывающим дискомфорт. Болезненность в левой подвздошной области с добровольной мышечной защитой. Симптом Щёткина-Блюмберга отрицательный. Перистальтика ослаблена. Пальпируемых образований нет. Симптом поколачивания отрицательный с обеих сторон.
cases.imaging_ru: КТ брюшной полости и таза с контрастированием: утолщение стенки сигмовидной кишки, воспалительные изменения околокишечной клетчатки, небольшое локальное скопление жидкости рядом с сигмовидной кишкой. Имеется дивертикулёз.

cases.chiefComplaint: Lower abdominal pain, no bowel movement for 3 days
cases.history: Patient reports lower abdominal pain for 3 days. No passage of stool or gas for 3 days. Associated with nausea and vomiting. Fever present. Patient appears weak and malnourished. Has not achieved menarche. History of fever 4 days prior to admission (treated at home as suspected malaria, no documentation).
cases.physicalExam: Thin, weak appearance. Dull affect but oriented. Abdominal distention present. Visible aberrant zone of transition in right para-umbilical region. Tenderness to palpation diffusely. High-pitched bowel sounds present initially, then absent.

labs.source: Intestinal Obstruction case report - exact values

cases.imaging: CECT Abdomen: Abnormal dilatation of small bowel loops (jejunum, ileum) measuring 4-5cm in diameter. Transition point at distal ileum with suspected adhesion. Collapsed distal bowel loops. Clumping of mesentery. Mild free fluid in abdomen and pelvis.
cases.keyDifferentiator: Dilated small bowel loops with transition point at distal ileum on CECT
cases.diagnosticCriteria: Case report - transition point visible at level of distal ilium, likely secondary to adhesion
cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Боль внизу живота, отсутствие стула в течение 3 дней
cases.history_ru: Пациентка сообщает о боли внизу живота в течение 3 дней. В течение 3 дней нет отхождения стула и газов. Сопровождается тошнотой и рвотой. Есть лихорадка. Пациентка выглядит слабой и истощённой. Менархе не было. В анамнезе лихорадка за 4 дня до госпитализации, лечилась дома.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Худая, слабая на вид. Аффект вялый, но ориентирована. Имеется вздутие живота. Видна атипичная переходная зона в правой параумбиликальной области. Диффузная болезненность при пальпации. Сначала выслушиваются высокочастотные кишечные шумы, затем исчезают.
cases.imaging_ru: КТ брюшной полости с контрастным усилением: патологическое расширение петель тонкой кишки (тощей и подвздошной) до 4-5 см. Точка перехода на уровне дистального отдела подвздошной кишки, вероятно из-за спаек. Дистальные отделы кишки спавшиеся. Скученность брыжейки. Небольшое количество свободной жидкости в брюшной полости и тазу.

cases.chiefComplaint: Severe epigastric pain radiating to back
cases.history: Patient reports sudden onset of severe epigastric pain 6 hours ago. Pain described as 'worst pain of life', constant, radiates to back. Associated with nausea and vomiting. History of chronic NSAID use for osteoarthritis. Denies hematemesis but reports black tarry stools yesterday. Smoker, 30 pack-year history.
cases.physicalExam: Appears in severe distress. Lying very still, afraid to move. Board-like rigidity of abdomen. Severe tenderness throughout abdomen, worse in epigastrium. No bowel sounds. Rebound tenderness present.

vitals.source: OSCE 2010 - Tim/Terri Travis Perforated Ulcer case

cases.imaging: Upright chest X-ray: Free air under diaphragm (pneumoperitoneum). CT abdomen: Perforation site visible in anterior duodenal wall, pneumoperitoneum, fluid in Morrison's pouch.
cases.keyDifferentiator: Pneumoperitoneum on upright chest X-ray with peritoneal signs
cases.diagnosticCriteria: OSCE 2010 source - Peritonitis due to perforated ulcer
cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Сильная боль в эпигастрии с иррадиацией в спину
cases.history_ru: Пациент сообщает о внезапном начале сильной боли в эпигастрии 6 часов назад. Боль описывает как «самую сильную в жизни», она постоянная и иррадиирует в спину. Сопровождается тошнотой и рвотой. В анамнезе длительный приём НПВС по поводу остеоартрита. Отрицает кровавую рвоту, но сообщает о чёрном дегтеобразном стуле накануне. Курильщик, стаж 30 пачка-лет.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Находится в выраженном дистрессе. Лежит совершенно неподвижно, боится двигаться. Доскообразное напряжение мышц живота. Выраженная болезненность по всему животу, максимальная в эпигастрии. Кишечные шумы не выслушиваются. Симптом Щёткина-Блюмберга положительный.
cases.imaging_ru: Рентгенография грудной клетки стоя: свободный газ под диафрагмой (пневмоперитонеум). КТ брюшной полости: визуализируется место перфорации в передней стенке двенадцатиперстной кишки, пневмоперитонеум, жидкость в кармане Моррисона.

cases.chiefComplaint: Right lower quadrant pain and fever
cases.history: 24-hour history of periumbilical pain migrating to right lower quadrant. Constant dull ache, gradually worsening. Nausea present, vomited twice. Anorexia since yesterday. Low-grade fever. Last menstrual period normal, 10 days ago. No vaginal discharge.
cases.physicalExam: Uncomfortable, lying still. Right lower quadrant tenderness maximal at McBurney's point. Guarding present. Positive Rovsing's sign. No rebound initially but develops with continued examination.

labs.source: BJR 2011 - Grade II appendicitis: WBC 12.97±3.73, CRP 0.99±1.63

cases.imaging: CT abdomen/pelvis: Fluid-filled appendix 8mm diameter. Enhancing wall thickening present. Subtle periappendiceal stranding. No abscess or free air.
cases.keyDifferentiator: Appendiceal diameter >6mm with wall thickening but only subtle periappendiceal stranding
cases.diagnosticCriteria: BJR 2011 Grade II: appendix >6mm with enhancing wall thickening with/without subtle periappendiceal stranding
supportingEvidence: Migration of pain from periumbilical to right lower quadrant is classic for appendicitis
Positive McBurney's point tenderness and Rovsing's sign strongly suggest appendiceal inflammation
WBC 12.8 x10³/μL elevated, consistent with acute inflammatory process
CT shows fluid-filled appendix 8mm diameter (>6mm diagnostic threshold)
Enhancing appendiceal wall thickening confirms active inflammation
CRP 0.95 mg/dL matches BJR 2011 Grade II range (0.99±1.63 mg/dL)
cases.clinicalReasoning: The patient presents with classic appendicitis progression: initial periumbilical pain (visceral innervation) migrating to right lower quadrant (parietal peritoneum involvement). Physical exam findings of McBurney's point tenderness with positive Rovsing's sign have high positive predictive value. Laboratory markers show WBC and CRP levels precisely matching BJR 2011 Grade II appendicitis cohort. CT imaging confirms appendiceal diameter >6mm with wall enhancement, meeting diagnostic criteria for mild appendicitis without perforation.

supporting: Appendiceal diameter 8mm exceeds 6mm diagnostic threshold (BJR 2011 criteria)
Enhancing wall thickening visible on CT confirms active inflammation
WBC 12.8 x10³/μL matches BJR 2011 Grade II mean (12.97±3.73)
Anorexia and nausea typical for appendicitis (98% sensitive)
challenging: CRP only mildly elevated (0.95 mg/dL) - perforation would show CRP >8 mg/dL (BJR 2011 Grade IV)
Only subtle periappendiceal stranding - moderate-severe stranding would suggest Grade III
No abscess or phlegmon formation - rules out complicated appendicitis
Fever only 38.5°C - perforated appendicitis typically >39°C

differentialComparison.differentiator: Appendiceal diameter 8mm with wall thickening matches BJR 2011 Grade II CT criteria exactly
Last menstrual period normal 10 days ago, no vaginal bleeding or discharge, appendix clearly visualized on CT
Pain localized to appendiceal region, no vaginal discharge, no cervical motion tenderness
CRP 0.95 mg/dL rules out perforation (Grade IV shows CRP 8.10±6.90 mg/dL per BJR 2011)

labs.wbc: 12.8 x10³/μL (elevated) - matches BJR 2011 Grade II mean of 12.97 x10³/μL
labs.crp: 0.95 mg/dL (mildly elevated) - matches BJR 2011 Grade II mean of 0.99 mg/dL

imaging.ct_findings: Appendiceal diameter: 8mm (>6mm threshold), Wall thickening with enhancement present, Periappendiceal stranding: subtle, No abscess or phlegmon, No perforation or wall defect

vitals_trend.note: Temperature 38.5°C consistent with localized inflammation without perforation

cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Боль в правой подвздошной области и лихорадка

argsFor: Pain migrated from periumbilical area to RLQ.
McBurney tenderness, guarding, and positive Rovsing sign present.
CT shows 8mm inflamed appendix with periappendiceal stranding.
argsAgainst: No abscess or free air on CT.
CRP only moderately elevated.
argsFor_ru: Боль сместилась из околопупочной области в правую подвздошную.
Есть болезненность в точке Мак-Бурнея, защита и симптом Ровзинга.
КТ показывает аппендикс 8 мм с воспалением и периаппендикулярным инфильтратом.
argsAgainst_ru: На КТ нет абсцесса или свободного газа.
CRP повышен только умеренно.

argsFor: RLQ pain and fever are present.
WBC 12.8 and CRP 0.95 are elevated.
Nausea and vomiting are present.
argsAgainst: CT identifies an abnormal fluid-filled appendix.
Positive Rovsing sign favors appendiceal irritation.
McBurney tenderness localizes to the appendix.
argsFor_ru: Есть боль в правой подвздошной области и лихорадка.
WBC 12.8 и CRP 0.95 повышены.
Есть тошнота и рвота.
argsAgainst_ru: КТ выявляет изменённый жидкостный аппендикс.
Положительный симптом Ровзинга говорит за аппендикулярное раздражение.
Болезненность в точке Мак-Бурнея локализует процесс в аппендиксе.

argsFor: RLQ pain with nausea and vomiting is present.
Guarding and developing rebound indicate significant pain.
Symptoms began within 24 hours.
argsAgainst: CT shows appendiceal wall thickening, not ureteral stone.
Fever 38.5°C and elevated CRP favor inflammation.
McBurney tenderness and Rovsing sign are atypical for ureterolithiasis.
argsFor_ru: Есть боль справа внизу живота с тошнотой и рвотой.
Защита и нарастающий симптом раздражения брюшины отражают сильную боль.
Симптомы начались в течение 24 часов.
argsAgainst_ru: КТ показывает утолщение стенки аппендикса, а не камень мочеточника.
Температура 38.5°C и повышенный CRP говорят за воспаление.
Точка Мак-Бурнея и симптом Ровзинга нетипичны для уретеролитиаза.

cases.chiefComplaint: Right upper abdominal pain with chills
cases.history: Onset of right upper quadrant pain 18 hours ago, constant and worsening. Episode of shaking chills 4 hours ago. Feels feverish. Pain radiates to right shoulder area. Nausea present, no vomiting. Last ate 24 hours ago (Chinese food). History of similar episode 4 months ago that resolved on its own. Noticed dark urine this morning.
cases.physicalExam: Moderate distress. Lying on right side. Significant right upper quadrant tenderness. Murphy's sign positive. No jaundice visible. No abdominal distention.

vitals.source: Based on OSCE 2010 Cholangitis case - similar vital signs pattern

cases.imaging: RUQ ultrasound: Thickened gallbladder wall (5mm), pericholecystic fluid, positive sonographic Murphy's sign. Common bile duct 7mm (upper limit normal). Multiple gallstones present.
cases.keyDifferentiator: Charcot's triad (fever, RUQ pain, jaundice/elevated bilirubin) with dilated CBD indicates cholangitis, NOT just cholecystitis
cases.diagnosticCriteria: OSCE 2010 - Cholangitis requires bile duct involvement, not just gallbladder inflammation
supportingEvidence: Gallbladder wall thickening (5mm) exceeds normal threshold (3mm)
Pericholecystic fluid indicates acute inflammation
Positive sonographic Murphy's sign strongly suggests gallbladder pathology
WBC markedly elevated (16.2 x10³/μL) consistent with acute bacterial infection
Multiple gallstones present as nidus for inflammation
cases.clinicalReasoning: The patient presents with classic signs of acute cholecystitis: RUQ pain, fever, and positive Murphy's sign. Ultrasound confirms thickened gallbladder wall with pericholecystic fluid and gallstones. Laboratory shows elevated WBC and liver enzymes. Common bile duct at 7mm is at upper limit of normal. The clinical presentation with gallbladder inflammation points to acute cholecystitis as the primary diagnosis.

supporting: Positive Murphy's sign indicates gallbladder inflammation
Gallbladder wall thickening >3mm confirms cholecystitis
Pericholecystic fluid supports acute inflammatory process
History of prior similar episode suggests chronic gallstone disease
challenging: Charcot's triad present: fever + RUQ pain + hyperbilirubinemia (3.5 mg/dL)
Marked elevation of alkaline phosphatase (380 U/L) suggests biliary obstruction, not just cholecystitis
Dark urine indicates conjugated hyperbilirubinemia (cholestatic pattern)
Common bile duct 7mm is dilated for patient age - OSCE source shows cholangitis has dilated CBD >10mm typically
Shaking chills (Reynold's pentad component) more specific for cholangitis than cholecystitis
Hypotension (94/62) and tachycardia (116) suggest systemic infection beyond gallbladder

differentialComparison.differentiator: Gallbladder findings support this, BUT doesn't explain marked hyperbilirubinemia or bile duct dilation
Charcot's triad + Reynold's pentad (shaking chills + hypotension) + dilated CBD indicates bile duct infection, not just gallbladder. OSCE 2010 source confirms cholangitis presentation.
Would expect epigastric pain radiating to back, elevated amylase/lipase (not measured here)

labs.alkaline_phosphatase: 380 U/L - disproportionately elevated vs ALT suggests biliary obstruction
labs.note: Cholestatic pattern with high Alk Phos indicates bile duct pathology, not simple cholecystitis

imaging.cbd_measurement: 7mm (borderline dilated - OSCE source indicates cholangitis typically shows >10-12mm dilation)
imaging.gallbladder: Wall thickening present, but key question: is infection confined to gallbladder or involving bile duct?
imaging.clinical_correlation: Hyperbilirubinemia (3.5 mg/dL) cannot be explained by cholecystitis alone - requires bile duct obstruction

clinical_signs.charcot_triad: All three present: Fever (102.2°F), RUQ pain, Jaundice (bili 3.5)
clinical_signs.reynold_pentad: Triad + Hypotension (94/62) + Altered mentation (lethargy from distress)
clinical_signs.source: OSCE 2010 Cholangitis case - Charcot's triad is diagnostic criterion

cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Боль в правом подреберье с ознобом
cases.history_ru: Боль в правом подреберье началась 18 часов назад, постоянная и усиливающаяся. 4 часа назад был эпизод потрясающего озноба. Чувствует лихорадку. Боль иррадиирует в область правого плеча. Есть тошнота, рвоты нет. Последний приём пищи был 24 часа назад. Похожий эпизод был 4 месяца назад и прошёл самостоятельно. Утром заметил тёмную мочу.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Умеренно тяжёлое состояние. Лежит на правом боку. Выраженная болезненность в правом подреберье. Симптом Мёрфи положительный. Видимой желтухи нет. Вздутия живота нет.
cases.imaging_ru: УЗИ правого подреберья: утолщение стенки желчного пузыря до 5 мм, перихолецистическая жидкость, положительный сонографический симптом Мёрфи. Общий желчный проток 7 мм (верхняя граница нормы). Имеются множественные камни в желчном пузыре.

The clinical presentation with gallbladder inflammation points to acute cholecystitis as the primary diagnosis.

cases.clinicalReasoning: Patient presents with progression from simple to complicated appendicitis. The 48-hour duration with increasing severity, high fever, and peritoneal signs (rebound, guarding) indicate advancement beyond early appendicitis. Laboratory values precisely match BJR 2011 Grade III cohort. CT demonstrates Grade III criteria: appendiceal diameter >6mm with moderate periappendiceal stranding but crucially no perforation (no wall defect, abscess, or free fluid). This represents localized peritonitis requiring urgent surgical intervention.

cases.chiefComplaint: Right upper quadrant pain, fever, and dark urine
cases.history: A 56-year-old woman presented with 24 hours of worsening right upper quadrant pain, nausea, fever, shaking chills, and dark urine. She reported two prior self-limited episodes of post-prandial right upper abdominal pain over the past 6 months. The current pain was constant, radiated to the right shoulder blade, and was more severe than the earlier episodes.
cases.physicalExam: Ill-appearing and lying on her right side. Marked right upper quadrant tenderness. Mild scleral icterus visible on close inspection. Murphy sign difficult to assess because of pain. No generalized peritonitis or abdominal distention.

labs.source: OSCE 2010 - cholangitis case values used in the file dataset

cases.imaging: Right upper quadrant ultrasound: Dilated common bile duct measuring 12 mm, gallstones present, thickened gallbladder wall, and positive sonographic Murphy sign.
cases.additionalFindings: Dark urine and pale stools reported by the patient.
cases.keyDifferentiator: Fever, right upper quadrant pain, jaundice/cholestatic labs, and a dilated common bile duct indicate bile duct infection rather than isolated gallbladder inflammation.
cases.diagnosticCriteria: OSCE 2010 source: Charcot triad with cholestatic liver tests and dilated common bile duct on ultrasound.
supportingEvidence: Fever, chills, and right upper quadrant pain fit acute biliary sepsis
Bilirubin 3.2 mg/dL and alkaline phosphatase 420 U/L indicate obstructive cholestatic pattern
Common bile duct is dilated to 12 mm on ultrasound
Gallstones provide the likely obstructing source
Hypotension and tachycardia indicate systemic illness rather than simple biliary colic
cases.clinicalReasoning: This is a straightforward cholangitis case built from the same OSCE source already used elsewhere in the file. The diagnosis becomes secure when local biliary pain is paired with systemic infection and objective evidence of duct obstruction. The key teaching point is to distinguish ascending cholangitis from acute cholecystitis: in cholangitis the duct is involved, bilirubin and alkaline phosphatase rise, and the patient appears septic.

supporting: Marked fever with shaking chills suggests bacteremia
Total bilirubin 3.2 mg/dL supports jaundice even if subtle on exam
Alkaline phosphatase 420 U/L indicates obstructive pattern
CBD dilation to 12 mm confirms duct involvement
challenging: Gallbladder wall thickening and Murphy sign can tempt the reader toward cholecystitis
However isolated cholecystitis does not adequately explain bilirubin 3.2 mg/dL plus 12 mm CBD dilation
The patient is hypotensive, which is more concerning for cholangitis with systemic infection

differentialComparison.differentiator: Charcot triad with cholestatic labs and a 12 mm CBD on ultrasound
Could explain RUQ pain and Murphy sign, but not the degree of bilirubin elevation and duct dilation
Biliary colic does not produce sepsis physiology or cholestatic laboratory abnormalities
Pain pattern and ultrasound findings point to the bile duct rather than the pancreas

labs.interpretation: Obstructive biliary infection with systemic inflammatory response

cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Боль в правом подреберье, лихорадка и тёмная моча
cases.history_ru: 56-летняя женщина поступила с 24-часовым анамнезом усиливающейся боли в правом подреберье, тошноты, лихорадки, потрясающего озноба и тёмной мочи. За последние 6 месяцев у неё было два самостоятельнo прошедших эпизода боли в правой верхней части живота после еды. cases.clinicalReasoning: Patient presents with classic perforated appendicitis complicated by abscess formation. The three-day duration with progressive symptoms, high fever, and systemic toxicity indicate advanced disease. Laboratory findings precisely match BJR 2011 Grade IV cohort: WBC 12.2 (Grade IV mean 12.85) and critically, CRP 8.5 mg/dL matching Grade IV mean of 8.10 mg/dL (significantly higher than Grade III at 2.39 mg/dL, p<0.001 per BJR study). CT confirms Grade IV diagnostic criteria: wall defect plus abscess formation. Requires percutaneous drainage and interval appendectomy.

challenging: WBC 12.2 x10³/μL not as elevated as expected - but BJR 2011 notes WBC doesn't correlate strongly with severity (r=0.222)

cases.chiefComplaint: Epigastric pain with repeated vomiting
cases.history: A 15-year-old boy was brought in by his mother for acute abdominal pain and vomiting. He had been in his usual state of health until the previous evening, when he developed epigastric pain and approximately 10 episodes of vomiting. He denied sick contacts, fevers, chills, chest pain, shortness of breath, diarrhea, constipation, headache, or focal weakness. The initial differential included food poisoning, gastroenteritis, Crohn ileitis, complicated cecal diverticulum, peptic ulcer disease, and mesenteric adenitis.
cases.physicalExam: Alert and cooperative but appeared uncomfortable. Percussion over McBurney's point elicited tenderness. No initial evidence of perforation, abscess, or bowel obstruction at presentation.

vitals.source: Patel et al., Radiology Case Reports 2025 - Acute appendicitis masquerading as food poisoning

labs.source: Patel et al., Radiology Case Reports 2025 - Table 1

cases.imaging: CT abdomen/pelvis: Dilated fluid-filled appendix measuring 8 mm, consistent with acute appendicitis. The report conclusion also described a trace amount of pelvic free fluid anterior to the rectum. No perforation, abscess, or bowel obstruction was present at initial presentation.
cases.keyDifferentiator: McBurney's point tenderness plus leukocytosis/neutrophilia and an 8 mm fluid-filled appendix on CT outweigh the misleading epigastric pain/vomiting presentation.
cases.diagnosticCriteria: Patel et al. case report: epigastric pain and vomiting initially mimicked food poisoning, but McBurney tenderness, WBC 13.7 x10³/μL with neutrophilia, and CT visualization of an 8 mm fluid-filled appendix confirmed appendicitis.
supportingEvidence: McBurney's point tenderness was present on exam
WBC was 13.7 x10³/μL with neutrophil count 11.2 x10³/μL and neutrophils 81.6%
CT directly showed a dilated fluid-filled appendix measuring 8 mm
No alternative source of vomiting or epigastric pain was identified on work-up
The patient underwent appendectomy with uncomplicated recovery
cases.clinicalReasoning: This is a deliberately deceptive appendicitis presentation. The history starts like food poisoning or viral gastroenteritis because the pain is epigastric and vomiting is prominent. The case turns surgical when the exam localizes to McBurney's point, the CBC shows leukocytosis with neutrophilia, and CT demonstrates a dilated 8 mm fluid-filled appendix. For senior learners, the key lesson is that atypical pain location early in the course does not neutralize objective surgical data.

cases.chiefComplaint: Lower abdominal pain for 1 week
cases.history: Gradual onset of lower abdominal pain 1 week ago. Initially came in waves lasting about 30 minutes, occurred every 2-3 hours. Sometimes awakens from sleep. Helps if gets up and walks around. Every day got worse. Yesterday morning when woke up, pain became constant, more severe, made worse by movement. Stayed home from work yesterday and rested in bed all day, staying as still as possible. Last night felt feverish, checked temperature which was 101°F. Took 2 Tylenol and went to sleep. Pain currently sharp and steady, made worse by moving and sneezing (has spring allergies). Has had poor appetite all week, no solids since yesterday (not hungry). Able to keep down ginger ale and bouillon. Had few loose stools when pain started a week ago, but now feels constipated and has not had bowel movement for 2 days. Had two prior similar episodes over past 2 years (2-3 times total) that lasted few hours and were much less severe. They seem to subside after bowel movement.
cases.physicalExam: Lying on back on exam table with legs extended. Some discomfort, lying still. Preoccupied with pain. Cooperative. Left lower quadrant tenderness present. No rebound. Mild guarding in LLQ.

cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Боль внизу живота в течение 1 недели
cases.history_ru: Постепенное начало боли внизу живота 1 неделю назад. Сначала она возникала волнами по 30 минут каждые 2-3 часа, иногда будила ночью. Облегчалась при ходьбе. С каждым днём становилась сильнее. Со вчерашнего утра боль стала постоянной и усиливается при движении. Накануне вечером была температура 101°F. Аппетит снижен всю неделю, со вчерашнего дня твёрдую пищу не ест. В начале заболевания был жидкий стул, теперь ощущает запор и не было дефекации 2 дня. За последние 2 года было 2-3 похожих, но значительно более лёгких эпизода, проходивших после дефекации.

cases.history: Progressive right lower quadrant pain starting 36 hours ago. Initially periumbilical, migrated to RLQ after 8-10 hours. Constant pain, gradually worsening. Associated with nausea and multiple episodes of vomiting. Anorexia present. High fever developed yesterday evening. Pain now severe, rated 9/10. No diarrhea. Last menstrual period 3 weeks ago. No urinary symptoms. No prior similar episodes.

cases.clinicalReasoning: Patient presents with severe acute appendicitis: marked RLQ pain, high fever, peritoneal signs, elevated WBC/CRP. CT shows severely inflamed appendix (10mm) with extensive periappendiceal stranding and free fluid. These findings raise concern for perforation (Grade IV). However, BJR 2011 grading system is explicit: Grade IV requires visible appendiceal wall defect with phlegmon or abscess formation. Careful review of CT shows appendiceal wall appears intact with no visible defect. Free fluid alone is not diagnostic of perforation - can occur with severe localized peritonitis (Grade III). CRP 5.8 mg/dL is between Grade III (mean 2.39) and Grade IV (mean 8.10), consistent with severe Grade III rather than perforated Grade IV. Correct diagnosis: Grade III (severe appendicitis with localized peritonitis, no perforation).

cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Боль в правой подвздошной области в течение 36 часов
cases.history_ru: Прогрессирующая боль в правой подвздошной области началась 36 часов назад. Сначала была околопупочной, через 8-10 часов сместилась в правую подвздошную область. Боль постоянная, постепенно усиливающаяся. Сопровождается тошнотой и многократной рвотой. Есть анорексия. Высокая температура появилась вечером накануне. Сейчас боль сильная, 9/10. Диареи нет. Последняя менструация 3 недели назад. Мочевых симптомов нет. Подобных эпизодов раньше не было.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Выглядит остро больной. Лежит неподвижно, бережёт живот. Выраженная болезненность в правой подвздошной области в точке Мак-Бурнея. Положительный симптом Ровзинга. Есть добровольная мышечная защита. Положительный симптом поясничной мышцы. Положительный симптом Щёткина-Блюмберга.
cases.imaging_ru: КТ брюшной полости и таза: диаметр аппендикса 10 мм, выраженное усиление стенки. Резко выраженные периаппендикулярные воспалительные изменения с распространением в правый латеральный канал. Небольшое количество свободной жидкости в тазу. Стенка аппендикса выглядит интактной, видимого дефекта нет. Дискретного абсцесса или флегмоны нет. Свободного газа нет.

cases.clinicalReasoning_ru: Patient presents with severe acute appendicitis: marked RLQ pain, high fever, peritoneal signs, elevated WBC/CRP. CT shows severely inflamed appendix (10mm) with extensive periappendiceal stranding and free fluid. These findings raise concern for perforation (Grade IV). However, BJR 2011 grading system is explicit: Grade IV requires visible appendiceal wall defect with phlegmon or abscess formation. Careful review of CT shows appendiceal wall appears intact with no visible defect. Free fluid alone is not diagnostic of perforation - can occur with severe localized peritonitis (Grade III). CRP 5.8 mg/dL is between Grade III (mean 2.39) and Grade IV (mean 8.10), consistent with severe Grade III rather than perforated Grade IV. Correct diagnosis: Grade III (severe appendicitis with localized peritonitis, no perforation).

cases.chiefComplaint: Severe epigastric pain for 8 hours
cases.history: Sudden onset of severe epigastric pain 8 hours ago, described as 'worst pain ever'. Pain constant, radiates to mid-back. Associated with nausea and vomiting (three episodes). Unable to find comfortable position. History of chronic NSAID use for osteoarthritis (daily ibuprofen for 5 years). Smoker, 35 pack-year history. No prior similar episodes. Last meal was dinner yesterday. Denies hematem esis but notes darkblack stool yesterday.
cases.physicalExam: Severe distress, lying very still. Board-like rigidity of abdomen. Severe tenderness throughout abdomen, maximal in epigastrium. Rebound tenderness present diffusely. Bowel sounds absent. Patient reluctant to move or take deep breaths.

vitals.source: OSCE 2010 Tim/Terri Travis - Perforated Ulcer variant

cases.imaging: Upright chest X-ray: Free air under right hemidiaphragm (pneumoperitoneum). CT abdomen/pelvis with oral contrast: Perforation site visible in anterior duodenal wall. Pneumoperitoneum with free air tracking along peritoneal surfaces. Free fluid in Morrison's pouch and pelvis. No pancreatic inflammation.
cases.keyDifferentiator: Pneumoperitoneum on upright CXR with peritoneal signs - surgical emergency

cases.clinicalReasoning: Patient presents with catastrophic perforation of peptic ulcer. The sudden onset of severe epigastric pain ('worst ever'), board-like rigidity, and absent bowel sounds are classic for perforated viscus with chemical peritonitis. Upright chest X-ray showing free air under diaphragm is pathognomonic for perforation. CT localizes perforation to anterior duodenal wall. Risk factors prominent: chronic NSAID use (5 years) and heavy smoking (35 pack-years) per OSCE 2010 source. Normal amylase/lipase exclude pancreatitis. This is a surgical emergency requiring immediate laparotomy. Mortality increases significantly with delayed surgery.

supporting: Pneumoperitoneum on CXR is pathognomonic for perforated viscus (OSCE 2010 diagnostic criterion)

No evidence of cholecystitis (perforation site is duodenum, not gallbladder)
No evidence of appendicitis (perforation in upper abdomen, not RLQ)

cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Сильная боль в эпигастрии в течение 8 часов
cases.history_ru: Внезапное начало сильной боли в эпигастрии 8 часов назад, описывает как «самую сильную боль». Боль постоянная, иррадиирует в середину спины. Есть тошнота и три эпизода рвоты. Не может найти удобное положение. В анамнезе длительный ежедневный приём ибупрофена в течение 5 лет по поводу остеоартрита. Курильщик, 35 пачка-лет. Подобного раньше не было. Последний приём пищи был вчера вечером. Кровавую рвоту отрицает, но отмечает чёрный стул накануне.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Выраженный дистресс, лежит совершенно неподвижно. Доскообразное напряжение мышц живота. Выраженная болезненность по всему животу, максимальная в эпигастрии. Диффузно положительный симптом Щёткина-Блюмберга. Кишечные шумы не выслушиваются. Пациент не хочет двигаться и делать глубокий вдох.
cases.imaging_ru: Рентгенография грудной клетки стоя: свободный газ под правым куполом диафрагмы (пневмоперитонеум). КТ брюшной полости и таза с пероральным контрастированием: визуализируется место перфорации в передней стенке двенадцатиперстной кишки. Пневмоперитонеум, свободный газ по брюшине, свободная жидкость в кармане Моррисона и малом тазу. Признаков воспаления поджелудочной железы нет.

cases.chiefComplaint: Abdominal pain, nausea, and no bowel movement for 3 days
cases.history: Gradual onset of crampy abdominal pain 3 days ago. Initially intermittent, now constant. No passage of stool or gas for 3 days. Multiple episodes of vomiting (bilious). Unable to tolerate oral intake. Abdomen progressively distending. History of appendectomy 5 years ago. No prior bowel surgeries besides appendectomy. No hernias noted.
cases.physicalExam: Uncomfortable, appears dehydrated. Abdominal distention present. High-pitched bowel sounds initially, then become intermittent. Diffuse tenderness to palpation. Minimal guarding, no rebound. Previous appendectomy scar visible in RLQ.

vitals.source: Based on Intestinal Obstruction case report - variant with prior surgery

cases.imaging: CECT abdomen/pelvis: Dilated small bowel loops in jejunum and ileum measuring 4-5cm diameter. Transition point visible at distal ileum with abrupt caliber change. Collapsed distal small bowel and colon. Moderate free fluid in pelvis. No pneumatosis or portal venous gas. No evidence of strangulation or ischemia.
cases.keyDifferentiator: Transition point on CT with proximal bowel dilation and distal collapse - classic SBO
cases.diagnosticCriteria: Intestinal Obstruction case report: dilated bowel + transition point + prior abdominal surgery
supportingEvidence: Dilated small bowel loops 4-5cm match obstruction case report criteria
Transition point visible at distal ileum indicates mechanical obstruction
Collapsed bowel distal to transition point confirms obstruction
No passage of stool or gas for 3 days consistent with complete obstruction
History of appendectomy 5 years ago provides source for adhesions
High-pitched bowel sounds indicate bowel attempting to overcome obstruction
cases.clinicalReasoning: Patient presents with classic small bowel obstruction: crampy abdominal pain, vomiting, obstipation (no BM/gas ×3 days), and abdominal distention. CECT demonstrates diagnostic features matching the obstruction case report: dilated small bowel loops (4-5cm), visible transition point at distal ileum, and collapsed distal bowel. History of prior appendectomy provides mechanism for adhesion formation - most common cause of SBO in developed countries. Normal lactate and absence of pneumatosis/portal gas rule out ischemia or strangulation. Initial management: NPO, NG decompression, IV fluids. Surgical consultation for possible operative intervention if no resolution.

supporting: Dilated small bowel 4-5cm precisely matches case report diagnostic criteria
Transition point visible = MECHANICAL obstruction (not ileus)
Collapsed distal bowel confirms obstruction at transition point
No BM/gas ×3 days = complete obstruction
Prior abdominal surgery (appendectomy) = adhesions likely cause
challenging: Lactate 1.8 mmol/L is NORMAL - rules out bowel ischemia/strangulation
No pneumatosis intestinalis - rules out transmural ischemia
No portal venous gas - rules out bowel necrosis
WBC only mildly elevated (10.8) - no evidence of perforation or gangrenous bowel
Patient hemodynamically stable - not strangulated obstruction

differentialComparison.differentiator: All case report criteria met: dilated bowel + transition point + prior surgery. This is mechanical SBO from adhesions.
Ileus shows generalized bowel dilation WITHOUT transition point. This case has clear transition point = mechanical, not ileus.
Would show elevated lactate, pneumatosis, or portal gas. Normal lactate and no ischemic CT findings rule this out.
CT shows dilated SMALL bowel with transition in ileum. Colon is collapsed = proximal (small bowel) obstruction.

labs.lactate: 1.8 mmol/L - NORMAL. Ischemic bowel would show lactate >4 mmol/L. This indicates bowel is viable.
labs.wbc: 10.8 x10³/μL - mildly elevated but not concerning. Strangulation would show WBC >15-20 x10³/μL
labs.interpretation: Labs reassuring - no evidence of bowel ischemia or strangulation

imaging.dilation: Small bowel 4-5cm diameter - matches obstruction case report exactly
imaging.transition_point: Visible at distal ileum - THIS is the key finding for MECHANICAL obstruction
imaging.distal_collapse: Collapsed bowel distal to transition = confirms mechanical obstruction at that level
imaging.ischemia_assessment: No pneumatosis, no portal gas, bowel wall enhances normally = NO ischemia

differential.sbo_vs_ileus: SBO has transition point. Ileus has diffuse dilation without transition. This case: transition point present = SBO
differential.adhesive_mechanism: Prior appendectomy causes peritoneal adhesions. Adhesions are #1 cause of SBO (60-70% of cases)
differential.management: Conservative trial: NPO, NG tube, IV fluids. Surgery if no resolution in 24-48hr or signs of strangulation develop

cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Боль в животе, тошнота и отсутствие стула в течение 3 дней
cases.history_ru: Постепенное начало схваткообразной боли в животе 3 дня назад. Сначала боль была интермиттирующей, затем стала постоянной. В течение 3 дней нет отхождения стула и газов. Многократная рвота с примесью желчи. Не может принимать пищу и питьё. Живот постепенно увеличивается в объёме. В анамнезе аппендэктомия 5 лет назад. Других операций на животе не было. Грыжи не отмечаются.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Испытывает дискомфорт, выглядит обезвоженной. Имеется вздутие живота. Сначала выслушиваются высокочастотные кишечные шумы, затем становятся редкими. Диффузная болезненность при пальпации. Незначительная мышечная защита, симптом Щёткина-Блюмберга отрицательный. Видим рубец после аппендэктомии в правой подвздошной области.
cases.imaging_ru: КТ брюшной полости и таза с контрастным усилением: расширенные петли тощей и подвздошной кишки до 4-5 см. Видна точка перехода на уровне дистального отдела подвздошной кишки с резкой сменой калибра. Дистальная тонкая кишка и толстая кишка спавшиеся. Умеренное количество свободной жидкости в малом тазу. Пневматоза и газа в воротной вене нет. Признаков странгуляции или ишемии нет.

cases.chiefComplaint: Diffuse abdominal pain with nausea and vomiting for 3 days
cases.history: A 67-year-old man with a 15-year history of arterial hypertension presented with diffuse abdominal pain accompanied by nausea, acid regurgitation, and repeated vomiting for 3 days. In the last few hours before arrival the pain worsened and spread to the epigastric region. He reported reduced appetite, last bowel movement 2 days earlier without pathological findings, and no smoking or alcohol use. Two days earlier he had a mild fever up to 37.6°C.
cases.physicalExam: The report emphasizes abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting with radiographic concern for ileus. Detailed abdominal palpation findings were not fully reported in the article. Surgical exploration later revealed a rotated gangrenous ileal loop with visible intussusception.

labs.source: Blažinović et al., Journal of Medical Case Reports 2025 - case presentation

cases.imaging: Abdominal X-ray in the left lateral decubitus position showed prominently shaped dilated loops of small intestine with air-fluid levels, suggestive of ileus. Emergency CT abdomen/pelvis showed ileus of the duodenum, jejunum, and proximal half of the ileum with luminal widths up to 47 mm. The ileum had wall thickening up to 12 mm with a transition zone demonstrating a target sign on coronal sections and a kidney sign on axial sections. Free fluid was observed interintestinally, paracolically, and in the small pelvis.
cases.correctDiagnosis: Ileo–Ileal Intussusception with Gangrenous Ileal Segment
cases.keyDifferentiator: CT showed both target sign and kidney sign at the ileal transition zone, converting a nonspecific obstruction presentation into a surgical diagnosis of adult intussusception.
cases.diagnosticCriteria: Blažinović et al. case report: adult diffuse abdominal pain with small-bowel obstruction on X-ray and CT evidence of target sign/kidney sign in the ileum, followed by operative confirmation of intussusception with gangrenous bowel.

cases.clinicalReasoning: For senior learners, this case is useful because the presentation is initially 'just obstruction' until CT identifies the mechanism. Adult intussusception is uncommon, so anchoring on generic adhesive small-bowel obstruction is easy. The decisive move is to read the CT pattern carefully: target sign, kidney sign, localized transition zone, wall thickening, and free fluid. Once those are present in an adult with leukocytosis and CRP 181.9 mg/L, operative urgency becomes the center of the case.

cases.chiefComplaint: Abdominal distention and no bowel movement for 3 days post-surgery
cases.history: Patient underwent laparoscopic cholecystectomy 3 days ago for symptomatic gallstones. Post-operative course initially unremarkable. Now complains of progressive abdominal bloating and discomfort. No bowel movement or passage of gas since surgery. Minimal nausea, no vomiting. Tolerating clear liquids. Post-op pain manageable with oral analgesics.
cases.physicalExam: Comfortable at rest. Abdomen mildly distended, soft. Diffuse mild tenderness without guarding or rebound. Surgical incisions clean and dry. Bowel sounds hypoactive but present.
cases.imaging: Abdominal X-ray (KUB): Generalized bowel dilation involving both small and large bowel. No focal transition point. Air-fluid levels present throughout. No free air. CT abdomen/pelvis: Diffuse small and large bowel dilation without focal transition point. No bowel wall thickening. No mesenteric stranding. Bowel enhances normally.
cases.keyDifferentiator: NO transition point on imaging - this is ileus (functional), NOT mechanical obstruction
cases.diagnosticCriteria: Paralytic ileus: generalized bowel dilation WITHOUT transition point, recent surgery, normal labs

Post-operative day 3 is typical timeframe for post-op ileus

cases.clinicalReasoning: Patient presents with post-operative bowel dysfunction 3 days after laparoscopic cholecystectomy. Imaging shows diffuse bowel dilation involving both small and large bowel. The AI recommendation of 'Small Bowel Obstruction' is a sensible foil because bowel IS dilated. However, the KEY DIFFERENTIATOR is the ABSENCE of a transition point. Mechanical SBO requires a transition point where bowel abruptly changes from dilated to collapsed (as seen in case report and INT_012). This case shows GENERALIZED dilation without transition - pathognomonic for paralytic ileus, not mechanical obstruction. Normal labs and recent surgery confirm postoperative ileus diagnosis.

labs.interpretation: Normal labs support benign functional ileus, NOT complicated mechanical obstruction

differential.management: Ileus: conservative (NPO, NG if needed, ambulation, time). SBO: may require surgery

cases.chiefComplaint: Severe abdominal pain for 8 hours
cases.history: Sudden onset of sharp upper abdominal pain starting at midnight last night (8 hours ago). Thinking it was indigestion, took several antacids and went to bed. Pain was unrelenting. At 2am vomited. Pain became so severe during night that patient and wife came to Emergency Room. Pain is constant, radiates to mid-back. Describes pain as 'worst pain of life'. Patient is truck driver, returned home last night from 3-day route. History of chronic NSAID use for back pain (ibuprofen daily for past 2 years). Smoker, 35 pack-years.
cases.physicalExam: Lying very still on back with knees bent. Obvious pain on face. Very still and quiet, not wanting to move at all. Not moaning but preoccupied with pain. Diffuse abdominal tenderness, worse in epigastrium. Rigid abdomen with guarding. Positive rebound tenderness. Bowel sounds diminished.

cases.clinicalReasoning: Patient presents with acute surgical abdomen: sudden severe epigastric pain described as 'worst pain of life', rigid abdomen with peritoneal signs, and hemodynamic compromise (tachycardia, hypotension). Two major risk factors present: chronic daily NSAID use (2 years) and heavy smoking (35 pack-years). Upright chest X-ray demonstrates pneumoperitoneum (free air under diaphragm) which is diagnostic for perforated hollow viscus. Given epigastric location, NSAID use, and clinical presentation matching OSCE 2010 template (Tim/Terri Travis case), this is perforated peptic ulcer (most likely duodenal). Normal amylase/lipase rules out pancreatitis. Requires emergency laparotomy for Graham patch repair. This is a surgical emergency.

argsFor_ru: Рентген стоя показывает свободный газ под правым куполом диафрагмы.
КТ подтверждает пневмоперитонеум и усиление брюшины.
Есть внезапная сильная эпигастральная боль с симптомом раздражения брюшины.
argsAgainst_ru: Прямой дефект стенки двенадцатиперстной кишки не описан.
Лактат повышен лишь умеренно.

argsFor: Severe upper abdominal pain with vomiting is present.
Pain is worst in the epigastrium.
Patient has tachycardia and fever.
argsAgainst: Amylase 105 and lipase 95 are normal.
Pneumoperitoneum is present on CXR and CT.
CT shows no pancreatic findings.
argsFor_ru: Есть сильная боль в верхней части живота с рвотой.
Боль максимальна в эпигастрии.
У пациента тахикардия и температура.
argsAgainst_ru: Амилаза 105 и липаза 95 нормальные.
На рентгене и КТ есть пневмоперитонеум.
КТ не показывает признаков панкреатита.

argsFor: Severe abdominal pain with mild lactate elevation is present.
Patient is tachycardic and hypotensive.
Pain is severe and unrelenting.
argsAgainst: Pneumoperitoneum strongly indicates perforated viscus.
CT shows peritoneal enhancement, not ischemic bowel findings.
Free air under diaphragm is incompatible with isolated mesenteric ischemia.
argsFor_ru: Есть сильная боль в животе с умеренным повышением лактата.
Пациент тахикардичен и гипотензивен.
Боль сильная и не прекращается.
argsAgainst_ru: Пневмоперитонеум убедительно указывает на перфорацию полого органа.
КТ показывает усиление брюшины, а не ишемические изменения кишки.
Свободный газ под диафрагмой несовместим с изолированной мезентериальной ишемией.

cases.chiefComplaint: Progressive post-prandial abdominal pain with acute worsening
cases.history: A 70-year-old man presented after 6 weeks of progressive post-prandial abdominal pain. Over the preceding 3 weeks he had nausea, vomiting, non-bloody diarrhea, food aversion, and 6.8-kg weight loss. Over the last week he became unable to eat, drink water, or take medications without severe abdominal pain; vomiting and diarrhea increased to 4–6 episodes daily. On arrival his abdominal pain was 8/10. Past history included hypertension, hyperlipidemia, myocardial infarction, coronary artery bypass grafting, peripheral artery disease, and recently diagnosed multiple myeloma.
cases.physicalExam: Cachectic. Abdomen was soft and non-distended without tenderness or guarding despite severe pain. On hospital day 3 he developed a newly distended abdomen with diffuse tenderness and guarding.

labs.lactate: 3.6 mmol/L initially; 13.2 mmol/L later; repeat 14.3 mmol/L

cases.imaging: Initial contrast CT abdomen/pelvis showed a distended gallbladder with mild wall enhancement and dilated loops of small bowel in the left upper quadrant. The CT report was later revised to note occlusive atherosclerotic disease at the origin of a combined celiac/superior mesenteric artery trunk. Repeat contrast CT on hospital day 3 showed extensive hypoperfusion of the entire small bowel and ascending colon with pneumatosis, dilated small bowel, and hepatic portal venous gas.
cases.diagnosticCriteria: Strait et al., JGIM 2021: severe abdominal pain initially out of proportion to exam, lactate elevation, celiac/SMA trunk occlusive disease on CT, followed by bowel hypoperfusion, pneumatosis, and portal venous gas on repeat CT.
cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Прогрессирующая постпрандиальная боль в животе с острым ухудшением
cases.history_ru: 70-летний мужчина поступил после 6 недель нарастающей боли в животе после еды. В течение предшествующих 3 недель были тошнота, рвота, не кровавая диарея, отвращение к пище и потеря веса 6.8 кг. При поступлении боль была сильной, но данные осмотра были скудными. На 3-й день в стационаре состояние резко ухудшилось.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Кахектичный. Живот мягкий, не вздут, без болезненности и мышечной защиты, несмотря на сильную боль. На 3-й день появился вздутый живот с диффузной болезненностью и мышечной защитой.
cases.imaging_ru: Первичная КТ брюшной полости и таза с контрастированием показала растянутый желчный пузырь с лёгким усилением стенки и расширенные петли тонкой кишки в левом верхнем квадранте. При повторной интерпретации выявлено окклюзионное поражение в области общего ствола чревной и верхней брыжеечной артерий. Повторная КТ показала обширную гипоперфузию всей тонкой кишки и восходящей ободочной кишки, пневматоз, расширение тонкой кишки и газ в воротной вене печени.
cases.correctDiagnosis_ru: Острая на фоне хронической мезентериальная ишемия

cases.chiefComplaint: Right upper abdominal pain after fatty meal
cases.history: Onset of right upper quadrant pain 6 hours ago after eating fried chicken. Pain constant, radiates to right shoulder. Nausea present, no vomiting. Mild fever. History of similar episodes in past. No jaundice.
cases.physicalExam: Moderate distress. Right upper quadrant tenderness. Positive Murphy's sign. No rebound or guarding elsewhere.
cases.imaging: RUQ ultrasound: Gallbladder wall thickening 4mm. Multiple gallstones present. Pericholecystic fluid. Positive sonographic Murphy's sign. Common bile duct 5mm (normal).
cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Боль в правом подреберье после жирной пищи
cases.history_ru: Боль в правом подреберье началась 6 часов назад после жареной курицы. Боль постоянная, иррадиирует в правое плечо. Есть тошнота, рвоты нет. Небольшая температура. В анамнезе подобные эпизоды после жирной пищи.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Умеренно тяжёлое состояние. Болезненность в правом подреберье. Положительный симптом Мёрфи. Симптомов раздражения брюшины в других отделах нет.
cases.imaging_ru: УЗИ правого подреберья: утолщение стенки желчного пузыря 4 мм. Множественные камни. Перихолецистическая жидкость. Положительный сонографический симптом Мёрфи. Общий желчный проток 5 мм, не расширен.

cases.chiefComplaint: Left lower abdominal pain and fever for 2 days
cases.history: Gradual onset of left lower quadrant pain 2 days ago, now constant and severe. Fever and chills. No bowel movement for 2 days. History of occasional constipation. No rectal bleeding.
cases.physicalExam: Uncomfortable. Left lower quadrant tenderness with guarding. No rebound initially. Bowel sounds hypoactive. No masses palpable.
cases.imaging: CT abdomen/pelvis: Sigmoid colon wall thickening. Moderate pericolonic fat stranding. Diverticulosis present. No abscess or perforation. No free air.
cases.diagnosticCriteria: OSCE 2010: Sigmoid wall thickening + pericolonic stranding + diverticulosis + elevated WBC/CRP
cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Боль в левой подвздошной области и лихорадка в течение 2 дней
cases.history_ru: Постепенное начало боли в левой подвздошной области 2 дня назад, теперь боль постоянная и сильная. Есть температура и озноб. Стула не было 2 дня. В анамнезе эпизодические запоры. Ректального кровотечения нет.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Испытывает дискомфорт. Болезненность в левой подвздошной области с мышечной защитой. Изначально симптом Щёткина-Блюмберга отрицательный. Перистальтика ослаблена. Пальпируемых образований нет.
cases.imaging_ru: КТ брюшной полости и таза: утолщение стенки сигмовидной кишки, умеренные воспалительные изменения околокишечной клетчатки, дивертикулёз. Абсцесса, перфорации и свободного газа нет.

cases.chiefComplaint: Abdominal distention, vomiting, and no passage of stool or gas
cases.history: A 41-year-old woman presented with 3 days of crampy abdominal pain, progressive abdominal distention, repeated bilious vomiting, and complete obstipation. She had undergone appendectomy several years earlier. Oral intake worsened the pain and vomiting.
cases.physicalExam: Appeared dehydrated and uncomfortable. Abdomen distended with diffuse tenderness. High-pitched bowel sounds were present. No rebound tenderness or generalized rigidity.
cases.imaging: CECT abdomen/pelvis: Dilated small bowel loops in the jejunum and ileum measuring 4-5 cm, a transition point at the distal ileum with abrupt caliber change, and collapsed distal bowel. No pneumatosis or portal venous gas.
cases.diagnosticCriteria: Transition point with proximal small bowel dilation and distal collapse on CT in a patient with prior abdominal surgery.
cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Вздутие живота, рвота и отсутствие отхождения стула и газов
cases.history_ru: 41-летняя женщина поступила с 3-дневным анамнезом схваткообразной боли в животе, нарастающего вздутия, многократной рвоты с примесью желчи и полной задержки стула и газов. Несколько лет назад перенесла аппендэктомию. Приём пищи усиливал боль и рвоту.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Выглядит обезвоженной и испытывающей дискомфорт. Живот вздут, имеется диффузная болезненность. Выслушиваются высокочастотные кишечные шумы. Симптом Щёткина-Блюмберга отрицательный, разлитой ригидности нет.
cases.imaging_ru: КТ брюшной полости и таза с контрастированием: расширенные петли тонкой кишки в тощей и подвздошной кишке до 4-5 см, точка перехода на уровне дистального отдела подвздошной кишки с резким изменением калибра, дистальные отделы кишечника спавшиеся. Признаков пневматоза кишечной стенки или газа в воротной вене нет.

cases.chiefComplaint: Sudden severe epigastric pain with abdominal rigidity
cases.history: A 61-year-old man developed abrupt severe epigastric pain radiating to the back. He had nausea, vomiting, chronic NSAID use for osteoarthritis, and reported black stool the day before presentation. The pain rapidly became generalized and movement made it worse.
cases.physicalExam: Severe distress. Abdomen board-like and diffusely tender with rebound tenderness. Bowel sounds absent. The patient lay very still and avoided deep inspiration.
cases.imaging: Upright chest X-ray: Free air under the diaphragm. CT abdomen: Perforation visible in the anterior duodenal wall with pneumoperitoneum and fluid in Morrison pouch.
cases.diagnosticCriteria: Peritoneal signs with pneumoperitoneum on upright chest X-ray and CT evidence of duodenal wall perforation.
cases.chiefComplaint_ru: Внезапная сильная боль в эпигастрии с ригидностью живота
cases.history_ru: 61-летний мужчина отметил внезапное развитие сильной боли в эпигастрии с иррадиацией в спину. Сопровождалось тошнотой, рвотой, длительным приёмом НПВС по поводу остеоартрита и чёрным стулом за день до поступления. Боль быстро стала разлитой и усиливалась при движении.
cases.physicalExam_ru: Выраженный дистресс. Живот доскообразный, диффузно болезненный, симптом Щёткина-Блюмберга положительный. Кишечные шумы отсутствуют. Пациент лежит неподвижно и избегает глубокого вдоха.
cases.imaging_ru: Рентгенография грудной клетки стоя: свободный газ под диафрагмой. КТ брюшной полости: визуализируется перфорация в передней стенке двенадцатиперстной кишки, пневмоперитонеум и жидкость в кармане Моррисона.
